blocks.text: Brackenridge Civil Group Inc is a civil engineering practice serving municipal and county public works departments, public water and sewer authorities, and private development clients. The firm's product is a sealed set of construction drawings and the specifications that accompany them, prepared by the people whose names are on them and read by the Principal Engineer before it leaves the office. We are a small practice by intention. The client who retains this firm deals with the engineer who does the work, and with no one else.

Our practice is site civil design in the ordinary sense of the term: what happens to the ground, to the water that lands on it, and to the pipe and pavement that have to coexist above and beneath it. We do not hold ourselves out as structural engineers, as environmental consultants, or as planners. Where a project requires those disciplines we say so early and in writing, and we coordinate with the specialist the client selects.

items: Site grading and earthwork design, including cut and fill balance, quantity takeoffs, and erosion and sediment control plans and narrative.

Stormwater management: drainage area analysis, hydrologic and hydraulic calculations, conveyance and inlet design, detention and water quality facilities, and the supporting narrative required for permit review.
Roadway design: horizontal and vertical geometry, typical sections, intersection detail, signing and striping plans, and maintenance of traffic.
Utility design: water distribution and sanitary sewer collection, gravity and pressure profiles, and the resolution of utility conflicts on paper rather than in the field.
Bid phase services: preparation of bid documents, addenda, tabulation of bids, and recommendation of award.
Construction phase services: shop drawing review, responses to contractor requests for information, periodic site observation, and record drawings.

blocks.text: Drawings are produced in this office on our own CAD standard, checked against a print, and issued under the seal of the Principal Engineer. Specifications are prepared to the owner's front end where the owner has one, and to the applicable state and county standard construction details where the owner does not.

A public body that retains this firm is buying a set of drawings that will survive review, a set of quantities the low bidder will not dispute, and a telephone call returned the same day. That is the whole of our claim. We do not promise a design that costs less than the ground will allow. We do not agree to a review schedule we already know the reviewing agency will not meet. Where we believe a scope is wrong, we say so during the proposal rather than during construction, when saying so is a change order and no one is pleased to hear it.

The firm is presently engaged by {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}}. That work is of the kind described above, and it is being performed in this office rather than brokered to a subconsultant and administered from a distance. A prospective client is welcome to speak with {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}} about our performance rather than accept our characterization of it, and we will make that introduction on request. We would sooner be judged on an engagement we can point to than on a list.

Mark Todd, Principal Engineer, is responsible for the technical content of everything this firm issues. He reads every agreement and every letter to a public body line by line before it is signed, and he signs them. Design and plan production are carried out by the firm's engineering staff to the standards described above, all of it in-house. Mark Barrett, Office Administrator, maintains the firm's project files, correspondence, and contract administration. Executed agreements are scanned and filed the day they come back countersigned, and a client who telephones for a copy of a document from one of our jobs will have it that day rather than after a search.

Inquiries regarding the firm's availability, its rates, or a specific project should be directed to Mark Todd, Principal Engineer. We will contact you promptly. Proposals are prepared at no cost to the prospective client and are written to the scope that was described to us; we do not publish a standard fee schedule and then negotiate away from it.
